agent_output:
```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "SHIP WITH FIXES",
    "one_liner": "Authorization bypass when unauthenticated, order ID enumeration via timing, and full object leakage on a public-facing endpoint."
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Unauthenticated access bypasses authorization check",
      "risk": "If req.user is undefined (unauthenticated request), userId is undefined. The check `order.customerId !== undefined` is always true, so the 403 fires — but only AFTER the database query executes. However, if customerId is ever null/undefined on an order record (e.g., guest checkout, data migration bug), `undefined !== undefined` is false and the check PASSES, returning the full order to an unauthenticated user.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "getOrder handler, lines 3 and 12",
      "reproduction": "1. Send GET /orders/:id with no auth token (req.user is undefined). 2. Target an order where customerId is null (guest order, incomplete migration). 3. The comparison `null !== undefined` or `undefined !== undefined` is false — authorization passes. 4. Full order object returned.",
      "fix": "Add an early authentication guard: `if (!userId) { res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }); return; }` before any database access.",
      "time_to_fix": "2 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Authentication and authorization must be enforced on all public-facing endpoints."
    },
    {
      "title": "Order ID enumeration via differentiated error responses",
      "risk": "The endpoint returns 404 for non-existent orders and 403 for orders belonging to other users. An attacker can enumerate valid order IDs by distinguishing these two responses — 403 confirms the order exists. On a public-facing endpoint this leaks business intelligence (order volume, ID scheme, active order IDs).",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrder handler, 404/403 branching",
      "reproduction": "1. Authenticate as any user. 2. Iterate GET /orders/1, /orders/2, ... /orders/N. 3. 404 = doesn't exist, 403 = exists but belongs to someone else. 4. Map valid order ID space trivially.",
      "fix": "Return 404 for both cases: `if (!order || order.customerId !== userId) { res.status(404).json({ error: 'Order not found' }); return; }`. This makes non-existent and unauthorized orders indistinguishable.",
      "time_to_fix": "2 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Public-facing endpoints must not leak existence of resources belonging to other users."
    },
    {
      "title": "Full order object returned without field filtering",
      "risk": "res.json(order) serializes the entire database record, which likely includes internal fields: internal pricing margins, payment tokens, customer PII (address, email, phone), fulfillment partner IDs, internal notes, audit timestamps. On a public-facing endpoint this is a data exposure vector.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrder handler, final res.json(order)",
      "reproduction": "1. Fetch any order you own. 2. Inspect response body. 3. Observe all database columns present including internal/sensitive fields.",
      "fix": "Map to a response DTO: `res.json({ id: order.id, status: order.status, items: order.items, total: order.total, createdAt: order.createdAt })` — explicitly allowlist fields rather than returning the raw record.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Public API responses must not leak internal or sensitive data fields."
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No input validation on orderId parameter",
      "description": "orderId is taken directly from req.params with no validation. Depending on the db driver, a malformed ID (e.g., an object, extremely long string, or NoSQL operator) could cause unexpected behavior or injection. Should validate format (e.g., UUID regex or numeric check) before passing to the query layer.",
      "location": "getOrder handler, line 2"
    },
    {
      "title": "No error handling around database call",
      "description": "db.orders.findById(orderId) has no try/catch. A database timeout, connection failure, or malformed query will throw an unhandled exception, likely returning a 500 with a stack trace to the public client — leaking implementation details.",
      "location": "getOrder handler, line 6"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Add rate limiting to this endpoint to mitigate brute-force order enumeration even after fixing the 404/403 leak.",
    "Add request logging with the authenticated userId and requested orderId for audit trail on this sensitive endpoint."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The auth bypass on null/undefined customerId is exploitable immediately in production if any order records have a null customerId.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — code-level review, no migration or state change.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Changing 403 to 404 and filtering response fields are client-observable changes. If any client logic depends on the 403 status code or on internal fields in the response body, this is breaking. Coordinate with API consumers.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging or metrics in this handler. Impossible to detect enumeration attacks or auth bypass attempts.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": true,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": [
        "Changing 403 to 404 alters the public API contract for unauthorized access attempts.",
        "Filtering response fields removes fields clients may currently consume."
      ]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert the handler to previous version. No database or state changes involved."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Auth bypass on undefined userId with null customerId — JavaScript semantics are deterministic here.",
      "Order enumeration via 404/403 differentiation is a well-known IDOR pattern.",
      "Raw database object exposure is directly observable in the code."
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether any order records actually have null/undefined customerId depends on the data model and business logic (guest checkout, migrations).",
      "Which specific internal fields exist on the order record depends on the schema."
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "req.user is populated by upstream auth middleware that may not run on all routes.",
      "db.orders.findById returns the full database record without field projection.",
      "The endpoint is marked as public-facing per the prompt, so it is internet-accessible."
    ]
  }
}
```

content.thinking:
Let me review this TypeScript API handler for security, correctness, and production readiness issues.

Hypotheses:
1. Information leakage via 404 vs 403 distinction (order enumeration)
2. Missing input validation on orderId (injection risk)
3. No authentication check - userId could be undefined
4. Raw order object returned may leak sensitive fields

Let me analyze each.
